parameters.description: Show absolute line numbers in the gutter. Helps with navigating to specific lines using G or : commands.
Show line numbers relative to the cursor position. Combined with number, displays the absolute number on the current line and relative numbers elsewhere.
Controls when the sign column is displayed. Set to yes to prevent the text from shifting when diagnostics or git signs appear.
Enable mouse support in the specified modes. Use a for all modes, or combine n (normal), v (visual), i (insert), c (command-line).
Synchronize the unnamed register with the system clipboard. Set to unnamedplus to use the system clipboard for all yank, delete, and put operations.
Time in milliseconds to wait for a mapped key sequence to complete. Lower values make which-key and multi-key mappings feel snappier.
Time in milliseconds of inactivity before the swap file is written and CursorHold events fire. Lower values improve responsiveness of plugins like gitgutter and LSP diagnostics.
Persist undo history to a file so changes survive closing and reopening a buffer. Requires undodir to be set for a clean undo directory.
Directory where undo files are stored when undofile is enabled. A common choice is ~/.local/state/nvim/undo to keep project directories clean.
Create a swap file for crash recovery. Disabling this avoids swap file warnings when opening a file already being edited.
Keep a backup file after overwriting. Most users disable this since version control provides a better safety net.
Make a temporary backup before overwriting a file. The backup is removed after the file is successfully written unless backup is also enabled.
Allow switching buffers without saving. Unsaved buffers become hidden instead of being abandoned, preserving their undo history.
Automatically re-read a file when it has been changed outside of Neovim and there are no unsaved changes in the buffer.
Do not redraw the screen while executing macros, registers, or other commands that have not been typed. Improves performance during macro replay.
Enable 24-bit RGB color in the TUI. Required for most modern colorschemes to display correctly in terminal emulators that support true color.
Show the current mode (INSERT, VISUAL, etc.) on the last line. Often disabled when using a statusline plugin like lualine that already displays the mode.
Show partial commands in the last line of the screen. Displays the count or operator as you type multi-key commands.
Number of screen lines to use for the command-line area. Set to 0 to hide it entirely (messages appear as notifications), or 2 to reduce hit-enter prompts.
Controls when the status line is displayed. 0 never, 1 only with multiple windows, 2 always, 3 single global statusline shared across all windows.
Number of spaces that a tab character counts for visually. Most modern configs set this to 2 or 4 to match their project style.
Number of spaces used for each step of auto-indent and the >> / << commands. Set to 0 to follow the tabstop value.
Number of spaces inserted when pressing Tab in insert mode. Set to -1 to follow the shiftwidth value. Allows mixing tabs and spaces gracefully.
Convert tab key presses to spaces. Enable this to ensure consistent indentation across editors and avoid tab/space mixing issues.
Automatically insert one extra level of indentation after lines that start a new block (such as { or keywords like if, for). Works best for C-like languages.
Copy the indentation from the current line when starting a new line. Serves as the baseline indentation behavior before smartindent or filetype indent.
Round indent to a multiple of shiftwidth when using >> and << commands. Keeps indentation aligned to consistent column boundaries.
When enabled, pressing Tab at the beginning of a line inserts blanks according to shiftwidth; elsewhere it uses tabstop or softtabstop.
Highlight all matches of the last search pattern. Use :nohlsearch or a mapped key to clear highlights after searching.
Show search matches incrementally as you type the pattern. The view scrolls to the first match in real time.
Ignore case in search patterns. Best used together with smartcase so that searches with uppercase characters remain case-sensitive.
Override ignorecase when the search pattern contains uppercase characters. Has no effect unless ignorecase is also enabled.
Searches wrap around the end of the file and continue from the beginning. Disable to stop searching at the bottom or top of the file.
Apply substitutions to all matches on a line by default, as if the g flag were always given. Adding g then toggles it off for a single substitution.
Enable magic characters in search patterns so that special regex characters like . and * work without escaping. Disabling requires escaping most metacharacters.
Wrap long lines so the entire content is visible without horizontal scrolling. Disable for a more code-editor-like experience with horizontal scroll.
When wrap is on, break lines at word boundaries instead of at the exact screen edge. Prevents words from being split across display lines.
Preserve the visual indentation of wrapped lines so that continuation lines align with the start of the text rather than column zero.
Minimum number of screen lines to keep above and below the cursor. Setting this to 8 or 10 keeps context visible while scrolling.
Minimum number of columns to keep to the left and right of the cursor when wrap is off. Provides horizontal context while scrolling sideways.
Highlight the entire screen line of the cursor. Makes it easy to locate the cursor position, especially in large files.
Highlight the screen column of the cursor. Useful for aligning text vertically but can slow rendering on large files.
Comma-separated list of columns to highlight with a vertical stripe. Commonly set to 80 or 120 to mark a line-length guideline.
Display whitespace characters using the symbols defined in listchars. Useful for spotting trailing spaces, tabs, and non-breaking spaces.
Define how invisible characters are rendered when list is enabled. Example: tab:>>,trail:.,nbsp:~ shows tabs as arrows, trailing spaces as dots.
Characters used to fill various UI elements such as window separators, fold text, and end-of-buffer lines. Example: eob: hides the ~ markers.
Controls how text marked with the conceal syntax attribute is shown. 0 shows all text, 1 replaces with a single character, 2 hides unless a replacement is defined, 3 hides completely.
Modes in which concealed text on the cursor line stays concealed. A combination of n (normal), v (visual), i (insert), c (command). Empty means concealed text is revealed on the cursor line.
Method used to create folds. Use expr with Treesitter for syntax-aware folding, indent for simple indentation-based folds, or marker for explicit fold markers.
Folds with a level higher than this value are closed. Setting to 99 effectively opens all folds when a file is loaded.
Initial fold level when opening a new buffer. Set to 99 to start with all folds open, or 0 to start with all folds closed. -1 uses the foldlevel value.
Maximum nesting level for indent and syntax fold methods. Prevents deeply nested code from creating an excessive number of fold levels.
Place new horizontal splits below the current window instead of above. Matches the mental model of content flowing downward.
Place new vertical splits to the right of the current window instead of to the left. Matches a left-to-right reading flow.
Automatically resize all windows to equal dimensions after splitting or closing a window. Disable to preserve manually resized window layouts.
Minimum number of columns for any window. Prevents windows from collapsing to zero width when many vertical splits are open.
Minimum number of lines for any window. Prevents windows from collapsing entirely when many horizontal splits are open.
Comma-separated list of options for insert-mode completion. Common modern setting is menuone,noselect for LSP completion plugins like nvim-cmp.
Maximum number of items shown in the popup completion menu. 0 means use as much space as available. Set to 10-15 for a compact menu.
Pseudo-transparency of the popup menu as a percentage (0-100). Higher values blend the menu with the text behind it. Requires a compositor-capable terminal.
Show a horizontal completion menu above the command line when pressing Tab for command-line completion.
Completion mode for the wildmenu. Common setting is longest:full,full which completes to the longest common string first, then cycles through matches.
Comma-separated list of file patterns to ignore during file-name completion. Example: *.o,*.pyc,__pycache__,node_modules to skip build artifacts.
A string of flags that controls which messages are shortened or suppressed. Append c to avoid showing completion messages; append I to skip the intro screen.
Internal character encoding used by Neovim for buffers, registers, and strings in expressions. Should almost always remain utf-8.
Character encoding for the current buffer when writing the file. Empty means use the value of encoding. Set to utf-8 explicitly for new files.
Line ending format for the current buffer. Use unix for LF line endings or dos for CRLF. This affects how the file is written to disk.
Comma-separated list of line ending formats to try when reading a file. The first match is used. Order matters for automatic detection.
Ensure the file ends with a newline character when writing. Disable if you need to preserve files that intentionally lack a trailing newline.
Indicates whether the last line of the file has an end-of-line character. Automatically set when reading a file; used by fixendofline when writing.
Controls what Backspace can delete in insert mode. The default allows deleting over autoindent, line breaks, and past the start of insert. Rarely needs changing.
Allow the cursor to move where there is no actual character. Set to block for visual block mode only, or all to move freely anywhere on screen.
Allow specified keys to move the cursor to the previous or next line when at the start or end of a line. Common addition: b,s,<,>,[,] for arrow keys.
Flags controlling automatic formatting behavior. Remove o to stop inserting comment leaders when pressing o/O. Remove c and r to stop auto-wrapping comments.
Maximum width of text being inserted. A non-zero value enables automatic line breaking at the specified column. 0 disables automatic wrapping.
Enable spell checking. Misspelled words are highlighted and can be corrected with z= or navigated with ]s and [s.
Comma-separated list of languages for spell checking. Neovim downloads spell files automatically. Example: en_us,en_gb for both American and British English.
Path to a file where words added with zg are stored. Allows maintaining a personal dictionary of accepted words across sessions.
Show the effect of a substitution command incrementally as you type. Set to split to also show off-screen changes in a preview window.
Present a confirmation dialog instead of failing when a command would abandon unsaved changes. Applies to commands like :q and :e.
